Name: Spatial n-back
Description: A continuous working memory task in which participants monitor the spatial location of sequentially presented stimuli and indicate whether the current stimulus matches the location presented two trials earlier (2-back).
Instructions: Participants viewed a red dot appearing one at a time in a 4×5 grid and responded using the keyboard whether the current location matched the location from two steps back.
Comments: Participants responded only after the third location was presented.
The task was administered on a black background with white text.
All inputs were collected from the keyboard.
